title: Slightly misleading error message on missing user session, not missing subuid/subgid
body:
When a user runs rootless podman without a proper login session, image pull fails with:

```
unpacking failed (error: exit status 1; output: 
potentially insufficient UIDs or GIDs available in user namespace (requested 0:42 for /etc/gshadow): 
Check /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid if configured locally and run "podman system migrate": lchown /etc/gshadow: invalid argument)
```

Even though it says "potentially", we can perhaps improve the error message in cases where the subuid/subgid mappings exist for the user. This happens when `fchownat` fails with `EINVAL`:

https://github.com/containers/container-libs/blob/e70c309aabae7d35bf386ff0165ef8c6315a5e26/storage/pkg/chunked/filesystem_linux.go#L175-L177

We could check for a user session similar to what c/common does(bus check) before printing an error message that instead suggests running `loginctl enable-linger <uid>` as the fix.

comments.body: why does the user session affect that?  As long as you are running with the correct uid:gid, then you can use `newuidmap` and `newgidmap`

right, the files are static and not depended on systemd so I see no relation to the user session.

Generally speaking even the error message as is already seems a bit optimistic, we are deep in library code that can be reached from many tools, buildah, skopeo, cri-o, etc... so suggesting a podman command here seems strange

I may have landed on an incorrect assumption, but I still can't seem to figure out why an EINVAL on fchownat was encountered when /etc/subuid and /etc/subgid were correctly configured for that new user (without a proper login session). A subsequent `loginctl enable-linger ..` fixed the problem, which led me to assume that perhaps the mappings were not set right inside the user namespace?  

> so suggesting a podman command here seems strange

Agreed, we _can_ drop the podman command from that error message.
is it something you can reproduce or did it happen just once?
